riskItems.description: Every risk entry, so a downstream document can cite RISK-xxx structurally rather than extracting the identifier from prose.
riskItems.schema: array<{ id: string, title: string, subsystem: string, owner: string, score: int, status: string }>

criteria.criterion: Every risk has a unique identifier. Duplicate identifiers corrupt every count and every downstream cross-reference.
Every score, likelihood, and impact traces to the retrieved data or the SQL results.
Each risk has title, description, subsystem, owner, likelihood, impact, score, status.
Risks are supported by the retrieved sources.

trajectory.description: Provenance checks against the recorded retrieval trajectory.

requiredSources.reason: Risk registers must be grounded in the controlled risk-management procedure, which defines the scoring scales.

forbiddenSources.reason: Must not be grounded in unapproved draft material.
